hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know to run your cineforum — from creating a round to crowning the winner.

steps.title: The complete workflow
steps.subtitle: Follow these steps in order and your cineforum will run like clockwork — from the first proposal to the final Oscar ceremony.

step01.title: Invite your crew
step01.subtitle: Build your cineforum team
step01.description: Before anything else, get your friends on board. As an admin you can create accounts for members directly from the Users section — they don't need to sign up themselves.
step01.bullet1: Go to Admin → Users in your cineforum
step01.bullet2: Enter email, name and a temporary password for each member
step01.bullet3: Share the site link and credentials manually — no automatic email is sent
step01.bullet4: Members can change their password from their profile after the first login
step01.tip: You need at least two members to make voting meaningful!
step01.details: The admin creates the account directly: enters email, name and a temporary password. No automatic email is sent — it's up to you to share the cineforum link (e.g. via WhatsApp or Telegram) and communicate the credentials to the new member. After the first login, the member can change their password from the Profile section. You can also disable a member at any time without deleting them, and re-enable them later.

step02.title: Create a round
step02.subtitle: Open a new season of movie picks
step02.description: A round is the container for everything: proposals, votes, and the final Oscar ceremony. Admins create rounds from the Rounds admin panel.
step02.bullet1: Go to Admin → Rounds and click 'New Round'
step02.bullet2: Give the round a name (e.g. 'Spring 2025') and set a date
step02.bullet3: The round starts in open state — proposals can now be submitted
step02.bullet4: Only one round can be active at a time
step02.tip: Rounds keep your cineforum history organised — you can always browse past rounds.
step02.details: The round is the heart of the cineforum: everything revolves around it. While a round is open, members can submit movie proposals and vote. When you close a round, all data is archived and becomes read-only. You can give rounds a thematic name (e.g. 'Halloween Horror', '80s Classics') to make your cineforum history more evocative. It is not possible to have two active rounds at the same time.

step03.title: Submit movie proposals
step03.subtitle: Everyone pitches their favourites
step03.description: With an active round open, every member can propose movies they want to watch. Search the IMDB database and add up to a handful of picks.
step03.bullet1: Navigate to the Proposal tab of your cineforum
step03.bullet2: Search for a movie by title — results come from IMDB
step03.bullet3: Select one or more movies and submit your proposal
step03.bullet4: All proposals are visible to every member immediately
step03.tip: Proposals are public — you can see what everyone else has suggested before voting.
step03.details: Each member can propose one or more movies per round. The search pulls from the IMDB database, so you'll find virtually any film with its original title, year, and poster. Proposals are visible to all members as soon as they're submitted — this creates a bit of healthy anticipation and discussion before voting. There's no hard limit on the number of proposals per member, but it's good practice to agree on a maximum within the group (e.g. 2–3 movies each).

step04.title: Vote with Schulze method
step04.subtitle: Rank movies, not just pick one
step04.description: Once proposals are in, members vote by ranking all proposed movies from most to least preferred. The app uses the Schulze method — a pairwise comparison algorithm that computes the most broadly shared ranking.
step04.bullet1: Open the Proposal tab while the proposal is still open
step04.bullet2: Drag and drop movies into your preferred order
step04.bullet3: Submit your ranking — you can update it until the proposal closes
step04.bullet4: The Schulze algorithm computes an aggregated ranking from all preferences
step04.tip: Schulze is fairer than simple plurality: it considers everyone's full preference order, not just their top pick.
step04.details: Here's how Schulze works: each voter orders all movies from most to least preferred. The algorithm then compares every pair of movies and counts how many people prefer A over B and vice versa. The result is an aggregated ranking that reflects the whole group's preferences. You can update your ranking as many times as you like while the proposal is open.

step05.title: Close the proposal
step05.subtitle: Admin picks the winner
step05.description: When everyone has voted, an admin closes the proposal. The app shows the Schulze ranking as a reference, but the admin manually selects the winning movie — which then becomes votable in the Oscars section.
step05.bullet2: Review the Schulze ranking to see the group's preferences
step05.bullet3: Select the winning movie from the list and confirm the closure
step05.bullet4: The chosen film becomes the proposal winner and appears in the Oscars
step05.tip: Closing is irreversible — make sure everyone has had a chance to vote first.
step05.details: When you close a proposal, the app shows you the Schulze ranking computed from the group's votes: it's a great starting point for the decision. However the admin has the final say and manually selects the winner from a dialog. This allows flexibility in case of ties or special situations. The selected winning film is then made votable in the Oscars section for rating votes. Closing is final and cannot be undone (unless the round is still open).

step06.title: Oscar voting
step06.subtitle: Rate the winning movies you've watched
step06.description: After watching the winning movies from the round's proposals, all members can cast their Oscar votes — personal couch-scale ratings that feed into the cineforum's all-time rankings.
step06.bullet1: Navigate to the Oscars tab of your cineforum
step06.bullet2: You'll find all the winning movies from the round's proposals
step06.bullet3: Rate each movie on a couch scale (1–5)
step06.bullet4: You can see everyone's ratings and the group average
step06.tip: Oscar votes are separate from proposal votes — they reflect how much you actually enjoyed the film.
step06.details: In the Oscars section you'll find all the movies that won the round's proposals — if the round had multiple proposals, there will be multiple films to rate. The scale uses couches as the unit: from 1 to 5 couches. All members can vote, not just the one who proposed the film. Oscar ratings feed the cineforum's all-time rankings: top-rated films, favourite directors, stats by country, and much more. They're completely separate from proposal votes.

step07.title: Close the round
step07.subtitle: Compute the winner and archive
step07.description: Once Oscar voting is done, an admin closes the round. The system automatically computes the round winner based on the average Oscar ratings, and determines who proposed it. Ties are possible.
step07.bullet2: Click 'Close round' on the active round
step07.bullet3: The system computes the round winner: the film with the highest average Oscar rating
step07.bullet4: In case of a tie, multiple films (and their proposers) share the win
step07.tip: Closed rounds are always accessible — browse your cineforum's full history from the Movies tab.
step07.details: When the round is closed, the system compares the average Oscar ratings of all the winning movies from the round's proposals. The film with the highest average becomes the round winner — and the user (or team) who proposed it is recorded as the round winner. If two or more films share the same average, they all win jointly. After closing, the round becomes read-only and all historical data remains accessible in the Movies, Rankings, and Stats sections.

step08.title: Notifications
step08.subtitle: Stay in the loop via Telegram
step08.description: Occupy the Couch can send Telegram notifications to keep members informed about new proposals, voting results, and round updates — no need to check the app constantly.
step08.bullet1: Admins configure the Telegram bot in Admin → Notifications
step08.bullet2: Members get notified when a new proposal opens
step08.bullet3: Everyone is pinged when a proposal closes and the winner is revealed
step08.bullet4: Round closure and Oscar reminders can also be sent
step08.tip: Notifications are optional but highly recommended for keeping the group engaged.
step08.details: To set up Telegram notifications, the admin needs to create a Telegram bot via @BotFather and enter the token in Admin → Notifications. The bot can be added to an existing Telegram group for the cineforum or send direct messages. Notifications cover the key moments: proposal opened, proposal closed with winner, round closed, and Oscar voting reminders. If you don't want to use Telegram, the app works perfectly without it — you'll just need to remember to check the site manually.

step09.title: Rankings & Statistics
step09.subtitle: Explore your cineforum's history
step09.description: As the cineforum grows, valuable data accumulates. The Rankings and Statistics sections let you explore who wins most often, which films impressed the group, and much more.
step09.bullet1: Movie rankings: most loved films by Oscar average, with comparison to external ratings (IMDB, Rotten Tomatoes)
step09.bullet2: User rankings: who has proposed the most appreciated films over time
step09.bullet3: Director and country rankings: discover the group's cinematic tastes
step09.bullet4: Personal stats: each member can see their own averages and history
step09.tip: Rankings update automatically when each round is closed.
step09.details: Rankings are available in the Movies, Users, Directors, Countries, and Timeline sections. You can filter by round or view trends over time. The Stats section shows aggregated data for each member: how many films they've proposed, how many times they've won, their Oscar average, and comparison with external ratings. Everything updates automatically when a round is closed — no manual action needed.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Still have questions? Here are the most common ones.
faq.a1: Any registered user can create a cineforum. The creator automatically becomes the admin and can add other members, manage rounds, and configure notifications.
faq.q2: Can I belong to multiple cineforums?
faq.a2: Yes! You can be a member of as many cineforums as you like. Each cineforum has its own independent rounds, proposals, and Oscar rankings.
faq.q3: What is the Schulze voting method?
faq.a3: Schulze is a ranked-choice voting algorithm. Instead of picking a single favourite, each voter ranks all candidates. The algorithm then runs pairwise comparisons between every pair of movies and finds the most broadly shared ranking. The result is shown to the admin as a reference for choosing the winner.
faq.q4: Can I change my vote after submitting?
faq.a4: Yes — you can update your proposal ranking at any time while the proposal is still open. Once an admin closes the proposal, all votes are locked.
faq.a5: The admin. When closing a proposal, they see the Schulze ranking computed from the group's votes and manually select the winning film. This allows flexibility in case of ties or special situations.
faq.q6: Are Oscar votes the same as proposal votes?
faq.a6: No. Proposal votes determine the Schulze ranking that the admin uses to choose which film to watch. Oscar votes are personal couch-scale ratings you give to the winning films after watching them — they feed into the cineforum's all-time rankings and stats.
faq.q7: Do I need a Telegram account for notifications?
faq.a7: Notifications are entirely optional. If your admin has configured the Telegram bot, you can opt in to receive alerts. The app works perfectly without notifications — you can always check the current state by visiting the cineforum directly.
faq.q8: Can a non-admin member close a proposal or round?
faq.a8: No. Only admins can close proposals and rounds. Regular members can submit proposals, vote, and cast Oscar ratings, but all administrative actions require admin privileges.

cta.title: Your first movie night is one round away
cta.subtitle: Create your cineforum, invite your crew, and let the democratic movie-picking begin.
